pr.title: Just about match ftCo_DamageIce and improve used names
pr.author: AlexanderHarrison

summary: Large fighter decomp cleanup centered on ftCo_DamageIce. The PR renamed several previously address-style functions and fields, substantially rewrote ftCo_DamageIce.c, matched ftCo_DamageIce_Collide and ftCo_DamageIce_InAirUpdate, improved ftCo_DamageIce_Init, and propagated clearer common fighter names such as grab_timer, ftParts_GetBoneIndex, ftCommon_InitGrab, ft_PlaySFX, and ftColl_HurtboxInit across many call sites. A reviewer note says conflicts with PR #1802 were fixed and a regression in ftCo_80091854 was addressed before merge.

key_files.reason: Main rewrite and matching target; renamed local helpers and reworked ice hurtbox/collision/jump escape logic.
Exported renamed DamageIce entry points such as ftCo_DamageIce_Init, ftCo_DamageIce_HitWhileFrozen, and ftCo_DamageIce_InAirUpdate.
Field naming changes used broadly by the fighter code, including grab_timer and DamageIce movement fields.
Common fighter type/attribute names were refined for DamageIce and grab-related behavior.
Replaced an unknown forward declaration with typedef struct ftHurtboxInit ftHurtboxInit.
Added forward declarations needed by renamed/typed library calls.
Updated symbol map with newly named functions including ftCo_DamageIce_* and common fighter/library helpers.
Updated scratch symbol names to match the new naming.

what_changed: Renamed ftCo_80090B48 to ftCo_DamageIce_OnHit, ftCo_80090B60 to ftCo_DamageIce_Init, ftCo_80091030 to ftCo_DamageIce_HitWhileFrozen, ftCo_80091274 to ftCo_DamageIce_OnHit2, ftCo_800914A4 to ftCo_DamageIce_InAirUpdate, and ftCo_80091620 to ftCo_DamageIce_Collide.

Reworked ftCo_DamageIce.c to use named concepts for ice collision and frozen-state behavior: ftCo_DamageIce_HurtboxOffset, ice_coll, wall_hit_dir, rot_speed, escape_timer, damageice_ice_size, damageice_min_speed, damageice_speed_mult_on_break, and damageicejump_escape_time.
Introduced a local inline helper ftCo_DamageIce_StartJump to consolidate repeated setup used when entering the DamageIce jump/escape state.
Replaced many uses of fp->x1A4C with fp->grab_timer across grab, bury, capture, shield-break, DamageSong, DamageIce, and related common fighter code.

Renamed common helper symbols and propagated their uses: Fighter_UnkProcessShieldHit_8006D1EC became Fighter_ProcessHit_8006D1EC, ftParts_8007500C became ftParts_GetBoneIndex, ftColl_8007B5AC became ftColl_HurtboxInit, ftCommon_8007DBCC became ftCommon_InitGrab, ft_80088148 became ft_PlaySFX, and HSD_Mtx_8037A230 became HSD_MtxQuat.
Added ftHurtboxInit as a named forward-declared type in ftCommon forward declarations, replacing an unrelated-looking unknown declaration.
Updated numerous character-specific files to call ftParts_GetBoneIndex and ft_PlaySFX instead of address-based names.
smart_moves: The PR paired matching work with naming cleanup, making the DamageIce rewrite easier to verify and easier for future contributors to build on.
It propagated renamed common helpers across all call sites instead of leaving a mixture of address names and semantic names.
The DamageIce code used named movement fields such as ice_coll, wall_hit_dir, rot_speed, and escape_timer to clarify control flow that was previously hidden behind x0/x4/x8-style fields.
The rewrite separated repeated frozen-to-jump setup into ftCo_DamageIce_StartJump, reducing duplicated setup in ftCo_DamageIce_Init and ftCo_DamageIce_HitWhileFrozen.
The PR used a named ftHurtboxInit struct and ftColl_HurtboxInit call for DamageIce hurtbox setup, improving type clarity around collision initialization.
Conflict/regression cleanup happened before merge: a collaborator explicitly noted conflicts with PR #1802 were fixed and the ftCo_80091854 regression was addressed.
decomp_lessons: When a common fighter field is understood, rename it globally. The grab_timer rename made many unrelated common states more readable and reduced future ambiguity around fp->x1A4C.
For fighter common actions, local semantic field names can expose state-machine intent: DamageIce x0/x4/x8 became wall_hit_dir, rot_speed, and ice_coll.
Renaming low-level helper functions is high leverage. ftParts_GetBoneIndex and ft_PlaySFX immediately clarify many character-specific call sites.
Do not limit symbol improvements to the C files; update symbols.txt and scratches.txt at the same time so search tools and future PRs converge on the new names.
Bot match reports should be checked for both wins and regressions. This PR initially had new matches and an unmatched-function regression, and review text confirms a later regression fix.
Near-match work is still valuable when it extracts structure. The bot reported ftCo_DamageIce_Init improved from 0.32% to 56.17% even though it did not fully match.
naming_conventions: Use subsystem/action names for common fighter functions: ftCo_DamageIce_Init, ftCo_DamageIce_Collide, ftCo_DamageIce_InAirUpdate, ftCo_DamageIce_HitWhileFrozen.
Prefer verb-object helper names for common routines: ftParts_GetBoneIndex, ftCommon_InitGrab, ft_PlaySFX, Fighter_ProcessHit_8006D1EC.
Keep address suffixes only when the behavior is still not fully identified, as with ftCo_80091854 remaining unnamed.
Use semantic fighter field names when meaning is clear: grab_timer instead of x1A4C.

Use action-prefixed common data fields for action-specific constants: damageice_ice_size, damageice_rot_speed_min, damageice_rot_speed_max, damageice_gravity_mult, damageice_dmg_time_reduction_mult, damageicejump_escape_time.
Use typed struct names for collision initialization data: ftHurtboxInit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The decomp.dev report credited three new matches: ftCo_DamageIce_Collide, ftCo_DamageIce_InAirUpdate, and ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys.
The same report showed ftCo_DamageIce_Init improved by 688 bytes, from 0.32% to 56.17%, but remained unmatched.
The DamageIce rewrite made collision branches explicit by selecting left wall, right wall, or ceiling normals and passing them to ftCo_DamageIce_Collide.
The code replaced raw matrix helper naming with HSD_MtxQuat when building rotation matrices from quaternions.
The PR used named hurtbox initialization data and ftColl_HurtboxInit instead of opaque struct/call names, which likely helped preserve layout-sensitive code while improving readability.
Evidence does not show handwritten assembly or explicit nonmatching pragmas in the excerpt.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increased by 1284 bytes (+0.03%), with new matches in ftCo_DamageIce_Collide, ftCo_DamageIce_InAirUpdate, and ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys.
Reported ftCo_DamageIce_Init improved from 0.32% to 56.17%, while ftCo_DamageIce_HitWhileFrozen initially regressed from 57.93% to 46.21%.
Fixed conflicts with PR #1802 and fixed the regression in ftCo_80091854; considered good to go.
Approved by ribbanya with no written review body.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for final matching or naming of ftCo_DamageIce_Init and ftCo_DamageIce_HitWhileFrozen.
Search for remaining uses of x1A4C or related grab_timer aliases to complete the field rename consistently.
Review ftCo_80091854 after this PR to understand the regression fix and whether it received a semantic name later.
Search for remaining ftParts_8007500C, ft_80088148, ftCommon_8007DBCC, and ftColl_8007B5AC references to ensure all call sites use the new names.
Compare PR #1802 to understand the conflict area and avoid reintroducing DamageIce or fighter common regressions.
